text.title: Remote Workshop Toolkit Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the remote-workshop-toolkit methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Step 1 (plan): Pick workshop type; draft agenda blocks; pick canvas template.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the plan gate.

rule.statement: Step 2 (pre read and ground rules): Send pre-read ≥48h before; share ground rules on invite.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the pre read and ground rules gate.

rule.statement: Step 3 (breakouts): Build 4–6 person breakouts with all four roles assigned.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the breakouts gate.

rule.statement: Step 4 (time zone split decision): Compute coverage_pct; split or async-relay if <80%.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the time zone split decision gate.

rule.statement: Step 5 (run and read out): Run workshop; capture decisions + parking-lot items.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the run and read out gate.

rule.statement: When time_zones_count >= 3 AND coverage_pct < 0.8, do: Split into two sessions OR run async-relay protocol.
rule.rationale: Codified from the decision-tree verdict; this rule binds the gate condition to a concrete, testable action so the routing remains auditable.

rule.statement: When default, do: Send pre-read ≥48h before; require 70% acknowledgement.
rule.rationale: Codified from the decision-tree verdict; this rule binds the gate condition to a concrete, testable action so the routing remains auditable.

rule.statement: When workshop_format == hybrid, do: Enforce one-laptop-per-person; otherwise run fully remote.
rule.rationale: Codified from the decision-tree verdict; this rule binds the gate condition to a concrete, testable action so the routing remains auditable.

rule.statement: If preconditions for remote-workshop-toolkit do not hold, do not apply this methodology; route to an alternative as the decision tree suggests.
rule.rationale: Methodologies misapplied to wrong contexts create overhead without value; explicit skip preserves the agent's budget.
